summary: mnemonic-safety-check ships wallet/seed scanner with hardcoded GitHub Pages C2
details: The package advertises itself as a mnemonic-safety check but ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that together implement a credential/wallet harvesting toolkit with hardcoded exfiltration infrastructure. scanner.js issues HTTPS requests to https://ddjidd564.github.io (a non-publisher GitHub Pages host) and runs system reconnaissance via execSync (whoami, os.hostname(), os.userInfo()). wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem walks (fs.existsSync over wallet-related directories) and POSTs results to remote endpoints, alongside curl/ping subprocess calls. build-verifier.js similarly combines child_process, fs, os, and https with POST/https.request/https.get and an execSync('docker push...') call. The combined fingerprint — wallet-scanner naming, host enumeration, filesystem scanning, and a hardcoded attacker-controlled GitHub Pages destination — matches the credential/seed-stealer pattern. Installing or loading this package leads to host identifiers and wallet/credential-related filesystem contents being shipped to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
